episode_title: Melissa Bell, Aron Pilhofer, Mark Chonofsky & David Chivers: Chicago Public Media on Building AI Tools That Serve the Audience
transcript_file: 2026-01-27 - Melissa Bell, Aron Pilhofer, Mark Chonofsky & David Chivers Chicago Public Media on Building AI Tools That Serve the Audience.txt
summary_claims: Chicago Public Media has both public media radio assets (WBEZ) and a traditional print/web publication (Chicago Sun-Times), making it a unique institution in the Lenfest collaborative
The journalism industry had mixed results adapting to the internet's disruption and missed many opportunities; it is critical not to repeat that mistake with AI
AI translation reduced turnaround from 'yesterday's news tomorrow' (essentially last week's news) to much faster publication for Spanish-language content
Chicago Public Media's audio archive is so extensive they don't actually know how much they have

matches.theme_name: The Automation Paradox: What AI Replaces, What Remains Irreplaceable

best_quote.text: People's reluctance and fear is around people who've come to them with an AI solution to a problem that they don't have or a problem that is not well solved by an AI system.
best_quote.speaker: Mark Chonofsky

matches.summary: The episode covers transcription of WBEZ's massive audio archive, English-to-Spanish translation reducing turnaround from days to near-real-time, natural-language data querying for school datasets, automated weather/traffic reports, and rapid prototyping with AI tools. The guardrails against content generation are explicitly articulated.
matches.matched_text: People's reluctance and fear is around people who've come to them with an AI solution to a problem that they don't have or a problem that is not well solved by an AI system.

matches.theme_name: Trust on the Line: Credibility, Transparency, and the Audience Contract

best_quote.text: One of the both the opportunities that AI presents to us is better surfacing our humanity and better surfacing our authenticity. And a lot of times I think there's a tendency, especially in leadership roles, to be a little bit more buttoned up and not admit to mistakes... I think that this is a moment for us as leaders to reconsider that and to say we need to admit that we're humans and that we err and that there is going to be a learning curve for us just as much as there is for anyone else.
best_quote.speaker: Melissa Bell

matches.summary: The AI-generated book list incident (7 of 10 hallucinated books going viral on Bluesky and Stephen Colbert) is the trust crisis case study. Bell's response -- transparency, apology, and policy creation -- exemplifies leadership vulnerability as trust strategy. The emphasis on not generating content and the guardrails philosophy protect credibility.
matches.matched_text: one of the both the opportunities that AR presents to us is better surfacing our humanity and better surfacing our authenticity. And a lot of times I think there's a tendency, especially in leadership roles, to be a little bit more buttoned up and not admit to mistakes or or to not to be careful about how you how transparent you are with your audience. And I think that this is a moment for us as leaders to reconsider that and to say we need to admit that we're humans and that we err and that there is going to be a learning curve for us just as much as there is for anyone else.

matches.theme_name: The Ethics Infrastructure: From Principles on Paper to Practice in the Newsroom

best_quote.text: We're spending a lot of time ensuring that the role of generative AI specifically is that there are guardrails around it and that we are not in the business of generating content at any point, up to and including links. We're not interested in that. We're not focused on that. But around it is a space for us to experiment.
best_quote.speaker: Aron Pilhofer

matches.summary: The guardrails-before-development philosophy, the no-content-generation red line (including links), the AI policy created after the book list incident, and Chonofsky's self-described AI skepticism as practitioner illustrate the ethics-practice relationship. The incident where a Hearst freelancer violated policy demonstrates the practice gap.
matches.matched_text: we're spending a lot of time ensuring that that the role of generative AI specifically is that there are guardrails around it and that we are not in the business of generating content at any point, up to and including, you know, links. We're not interested in that. We're not focused on that. But around it is is a space for us to experiment.

matches.theme_name: Building the Machine: Technical Architecture and Engineering Decisions

best_quote.text: I wouldn't say that we have an AI product roadmap. I would say we have a product roadmap of which AI is a part.
best_quote.speaker: Aron Pilhofer

matches.summary: The decision to reject LLM inference for content recommendation in favor of traditional ML vendors, the natural-language-to-SQL query interface, the audio archive transcription infrastructure challenge, and MCP as a transformational integration enabler illustrate pragmatic technical decision-making. The build-vs-buy calculus is explicitly addressed.
matches.matched_text: I wouldn't say that we have an AI product roadmap. I would say we have a product roadmap of which AI is a part.

matches.theme_name: The Literacy Imperative: Upskilling the Newsroom for an AI Era

best_quote.text: People's reluctance and fear is around people who've come to them with an AI solution to a problem that they don't have or a problem that is not well solved by an AI system.
best_quote.speaker: Mark Chonofsky

matches.summary: Chonofsky's approach of building trust through small wins, not pushing AI solutions but waiting for genuine pain points, and the fellow cohort's self-organized biweekly working group illustrate organic adoption. The journalist suspicion of outsiders and the AI-solution-to-non-problem as a fear source are key adoption barriers. Demos-not-memos reflects no-code empowerment.
matches.matched_text: People's reluctance and fear is around people who've come to them with an AI solution to a problem that they don't have or a problem that is not well solved by an AI system.

matches.theme_name: Organizing for AI: Leadership, Strategy, and Institutional Transformation

best_quote.text: I think the journalism industry had mixed results on adapting to the internet's disruption of journalism and missed a lot of opportunity to take their journalism forward, their distribution forward, their community gathering forward with the new tools available to them. So I think it's really important now for us to not miss this new opportunity where everything is almost being rewritten in the technology space.
best_quote.speaker: Melissa Bell

matches.summary: Bell draws the explicit historical parallel to missed internet opportunities. The AI-integrated-into-product-roadmap philosophy (not a separate AI roadmap), the lean team (two product managers, one AI fellow), the flexibility to pivot from original grant proposals, and the CEO's leadership signal through vulnerability all address organizational transformation.
matches.matched_text: This is Newsroom Robots, the podcast where we explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and the news industry. I'm Nikita Roy. Data scientist, media entrepreneur, and one of the many founders currently building their ventures at the Harvard Innovation Labs. On the Newsroom Robots, I'm excited to bring you insightful conversations with industry experts about how AI. is impacting the way we do journalism. Before we begin, I want to take a moment to honor David Shivers, who you'll hear in this episode. David was the lead advisor for the Lenfest AI Collaborative and he passed away on January 1, 2026. And this conversation was recorded the previous month. David was deeply passionate about supporting newsrooms with AI. He cared about building real capability inside news organizations and he brought so much clarity and purpose to this work.

matches.theme_name: Power, Money, and the Platform: Big Tech and the News Industry

best_quote.text: I think the journalism industry had mixed results on adapting to the internet's disruption of journalism and missed a lot of opportunity.
best_quote.speaker: Melissa Bell

matches.summary: Chicago Public Media's participation in the Lenfest Collaborative (funded partly by Microsoft and OpenAI) represents collective action. The resource constraints of local public media and the dependency on grant-funded AI engineering talent illustrate the structural inequality between well-resourced and under-resourced newsrooms.

matches.theme_name: Reimagining the News Product: Personalization, Formats, and the Experience Layer

best_quote.text: I wouldn't say that we have an AI product roadmap. I would say we have a product roadmap of which AI is a part. AI is contributing and enabling us to conceive of and launch potentially God willing successful products that we wouldn't otherwise be able to launch.
best_quote.speaker: Aron Pilhofer

matches.summary: The audio archive transcription unlocking decades of content for new formats, Spanish-language content expanding audience reach, natural-language data querying as a new product pattern, and the content recommendation initiative all aim to reimagine how audiences access journalism. The dual-asset uniqueness of public media (radio + print) creates distinct product opportunities.
matches.matched_text: I wouldn't say that we have an AI product roadmap. I would say we have a product roadmap of which AI is a part. AI c is contributing and enabling us to conceive of and launch potentially God willing successful products that we wouldn't otherwise be able to launch.

matches.theme_name: Survival Economics: Business Models, Revenue, and Market Position in the AI Era

best_quote.text: I think the journalism industry had mixed results on adapting to the internet's disruption of journalism and missed a lot of opportunity.
best_quote.speaker: Melissa Bell

matches.summary: The local business outreach tool for underwriting, the lean two-person product team, the budget and time scarcity of public media, and the grant proposal obsolescence problem all address resource constraints. The dual-asset uniqueness of public media (radio + print) is framed as a strategic advantage.

matches.theme_name: Regulating the Revolution: Law, Policy, and Journalism as Accountability

best_quote.text: We're spending a lot of time ensuring that the role of generative AI specifically is that there are guardrails around it and that we are not in the business of generating content at any point.
best_quote.speaker: Aron Pilhofer

matches.summary: The guardrails philosophy functions as internal regulation, but formal regulatory frameworks and journalism-as-accountability-mechanism are not substantively discussed.
matches.matched_text: we're spending a lot of time ensuring that that the role of generative AI specifically is that there are guardrails around it and that we are not in the business of generating content at any point, up to and including, you know, links.

matches.theme_name: The Prototype Pipeline: From Experiment to Shipped Product

best_quote.text: I wouldn't say that we have an AI product roadmap. I would say we have a product roadmap of which AI is a part.
best_quote.speaker: Aron Pilhofer

matches.summary: The Slack weather/traffic integration as a quick trust-building proof of concept, the pivot from building a recommendation engine to buying from vendors, the demos-not-memos approach, and the flexibility to redirect grant proposals when vendors already offer planned solutions all illustrate the prototype-to-production journey.
matches.matched_text: I wouldn't say that we have an AI product roadmap. I would say we have a product roadmap of which AI is a part.

matches.theme_name: Data Privacy, Source Protection, and the Boundaries of Responsible Use

best_quote.text: I came to this job as really an AI skeptic and I think I still maintain a skeptical mindset. I don't use AI tools in at least the popular sense in my personal life at all.
best_quote.speaker: Mark Chonofsky

matches.summary: Chonofsky's personal non-use of AI tools outside work reflects the AI-as-work-only-tool stance, but data privacy and source protection are not substantively discussed. The guardrails against content generation implicitly protect data integrity.
matches.matched_text: I came to this job as really an AI skeptic and I think I still maintain a skeptical mindset. I don't use AI tools in at least the the the popular sense. in my personal life at all, use it extensively for writing code for work and in the ways that it's integrated into our workflow processes and in the ways that it can help our our journalists and and wider business.
